gold.rationale: The article builds a misleading impression that ivermectin has become a credible outbreak-linked therapeutic option for hantavirus, when the available source basis only supports that some social-media advocacy and sales chatter existed while officials said no evidence shows it works and supportive care remains standard. Edited images further make old or unrelated ivermectin material look like current, authoritative outbreak graphics, strengthening a false connection between the drug and the MV Hondius cluster.

atomic_points.post_summary: The post discusses the resurgence of public and medical debate regarding the use of ivermectin for treating hantavirus, sparked by an outbreak on the MV Hondius, contrasting official cautious guidance with growing online advocacy and consumer demand.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Public anxiety over the MV Hondius hantavirus cluster has quickly moved beyond travel advisories and emergency briefings into a familiar modern debate: whether an existing drug could be put to use faster than a new therapy can be developed.
atoms: Public anxiety exists regarding the MV Hondius hantavirus cluster.
The debate concerns whether existing drugs can be used faster than new therapies can be developed.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Ivermectin, a long-established medicine used in humans for certain parasitic infections, is getting fresh attention online and from some physicians as a possible option now being reconsidered as concern grows over how to treat severe hantavirus illness.
atoms: Ivermectin is a long-established medicine used in humans for certain parasitic infections.
Ivermectin is receiving fresh attention online.
Some physicians are giving ivermectin fresh attention.
Ivermectin is being reconsidered as a possible option for treating severe hantavirus illness.

sentence_level.sentence_text: A cover image shared alongside posts about the ship-linked outbreak has sharpened that focus, pairing discussion of the outbreak with institutional-style graphics about ivermectin’s medical history and a reformatted information card that says, “Doctors revisit ivermectin as hantavirus questions grow.”
atoms: A cover image was shared alongside posts about the ship-linked outbreak.
The image paired discussion of the outbreak with institutional-style graphics about ivermectin’s medical history.
The image included a reformatted information card.
The information card stated: “Doctors revisit ivermectin as hantavirus questions grow.”

sentence_level.sentence_text: The presentation has helped give the discussion a more formal tone.
atoms: The presentation of the image and graphics gave the discussion a more formal tone.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The outbreak linked to the expedition vessel MV Hondius has kept hantavirus in the headlines after international health agencies said the cluster involved Andes hantavirus, with cases and deaths reported among passengers.
atoms: An outbreak is linked to the expedition vessel MV Hondius.
International health agencies stated the cluster involved Andes hantavirus.
Cases of hantavirus were reported among passengers of the MV Hondius.
Deaths from hantavirus were reported among passengers of the MV Hondius.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That has driven a surge in searches, social media posts and physician commentary over whether repurposed drugs should be tested more quickly.
atoms: There has been a surge in searches regarding repurposed drugs for hantavirus.
There has been a surge in social media posts regarding repurposed drugs for hantavirus.
There has been a surge in physician commentary regarding repurposed drugs for hantavirus.

sentence_level.sentence_text: In those discussions, ivermectin has become one of the most prominent candidates because it is inexpensive, widely known to the public and already approved for human use against diseases caused by parasites.
atoms: Ivermectin is a prominent candidate in discussions about repurposed drugs for hantavirus.
Ivermectin is inexpensive.
Ivermectin is widely known to the public.
Ivermectin is already approved for human use against diseases caused by parasites.

sentence_level.sentence_text: U.S. health officials and international health bodies still say supportive care remains the standard approach for hantavirus.
atoms: U.S. health officials state supportive care is the standard approach for hantavirus.
International health bodies state supportive care is the standard approach for hantavirus.

sentence_level.sentence_text: They have not endorsed ivermectin specifically and continue to await formal data, even as frontline discussion gathers pace.
atoms: U.S. health officials and international health bodies have not endorsed ivermectin for hantavirus.
U.S. health officials and international health bodies are awaiting formal data on ivermectin for hantavirus.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Doctors posting online have argued that the drug’s profile makes it difficult to dismiss.
atoms: Doctors posting online argue that ivermectin's profile makes it difficult to dismiss.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Some have cited laboratory research in other viral settings and published literature describing anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, saying those features could make ivermectin relevant in a disease in which severe illness can involve respiratory decline and an intense inflammatory response.
atoms: Some doctors cited laboratory research in other viral settings regarding ivermectin.
Some doctors cited published literature describing ivermectin's anti-inflammatory properties.
Some doctors cited published literature describing ivermectin's antiviral properties.
Doctors argue these features could make ivermectin relevant for hantavirus.
Severe hantavirus illness can involve respiratory decline.
Severe hantavirus illness can involve an intense inflammatory response.

sentence_level.sentence_text: One physician commentator, paraphrasing a view that has become common in recent posts, said the question is no longer whether the drug shows biologic activity worth examining, but why low-cost repurposed options are so often neglected while authorities stick to narrow treatment pathways.
atoms: A physician commentator stated the question is why low-cost repurposed options are neglected.
The physician commentator stated authorities stick to narrow treatment pathways.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Those arguments do not claim proof, but they have helped push ivermectin from the edge of the conversation into what supporters call a serious near-term candidate for study.
atoms: Arguments for ivermectin do not claim proof of efficacy for hantavirus.
Supporters call ivermectin a serious near-term candidate for study.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That argument has found an audience in part because ivermectin is not an obscure experimental compound.
atoms: Ivermectin is not an obscure experimental compound.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The drug has a long history in human medicine and is approved in a number of countries for parasitic conditions such as onchocerciasis and strongyloidiasis.
atoms: Ivermectin has a long history in human medicine.
Ivermectin is approved in a number of countries for parasitic conditions.
Ivermectin is approved for onchocerciasis.
Ivermectin is approved for strongyloidiasis.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Its age and familiarity have become central to its appeal in the current debate: physicians know the product, pharmacies know the product, and patients recognize the name.
atoms: Physicians know the product ivermectin.
Pharmacies know the product ivermectin.
Patients recognize the name ivermectin.

sentence_level.sentence_text: In an atmosphere shaped by outbreak uncertainty, a cheap, established medicine with a broad reputation for safety in approved uses can seem more practical than waiting for a purpose-built antiviral that may take years to arrive.
atoms: Ivermectin is a cheap, established medicine.
Ivermectin has a broad reputation for safety in approved uses.
Purpose-built antivirals may take years to arrive.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Supporters say that practical case alone should be enough to justify faster public-sector evaluation.
atoms: Supporters say the practical case for ivermectin justifies faster public-sector evaluation.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Official guidance, though, remains cautious.
atoms: Official guidance on ivermectin for hantavirus remains cautious.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Public health agencies in the United States, along with international bodies, continue to say there is no specific proven antiviral treatment for hantavirus and that care focuses on early supportive treatment, including close monitoring, respiratory support and intensive care when needed.
atoms: U.S. public health agencies say there is no specific proven antiviral treatment for hantavirus.
International bodies say there is no specific proven antiviral treatment for hantavirus.
Care for hantavirus focuses on early supportive treatment.
Supportive treatment includes close monitoring.
Supportive treatment includes respiratory support.
Supportive treatment includes intensive care when needed.

sentence_level.sentence_text: They have not established clinical proof that ivermectin treats hantavirus.
atoms: There is no established clinical proof that ivermectin treats hantavirus.

sentence_level.sentence_text: In the current debate, advocates increasingly describe that position not as a final answer but as a sign that the research process has failed to keep up with clinical urgency.
atoms: Advocates describe the lack of proof as a sign the research process has failed to keep up with clinical urgency.

sentence_level.sentence_text: With the Hondius episode drawing more attention, some physicians and patient groups say regulators are under pressure to study repurposed drugs more quickly rather than wait for a larger crisis before starting formal trials.
atoms: Some physicians say regulators are under pressure to study repurposed drugs more quickly.
Some patient groups say regulators are under pressure to study repurposed drugs more quickly.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Consumer demand is becoming part of the story as well.
atoms: Consumer demand for ivermectin is increasing.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Online posts reviewed this week advertised access to ivermectin outside the standard prescription pathway, presenting the drug as something people may want to obtain in advance as concern over the ship-linked illnesses spreads.
atoms: Online posts advertised access to ivermectin outside the standard prescription pathway.
The posts presented ivermectin as something people may want to obtain in advance.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Critics view those offers as opportunistic, but they also highlight how quickly public demand can outrun the official pace of review.
atoms: Critics view offers of ivermectin outside standard pathways as opportunistic.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Across social media and health forums, users have exchanged dosing questions, links to telehealth-style vendors and clips of doctors discussing possible off-label interest.
atoms: Users on social media and health forums have exchanged dosing questions for ivermectin.
Users have shared links to telehealth-style vendors for ivermectin.
Users have shared clips of doctors discussing possible off-label interest in ivermectin.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That marketplace activity, uneven as it may be, has added to the sense that ivermectin is turning into a policy issue as much as a medical one.
atoms: Marketplace activity has added to the sense that ivermectin is a policy issue.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The visuals circulating around the debate have reinforced that impression.
atoms: Visuals circulating around the debate have reinforced the impression that ivermectin is a policy issue.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Advocacy accounts have attached historical recognition material and scientific-themed imagery to posts about the MV Hondius cluster, creating a smooth visual link between the drug’s established place in medicine and current hantavirus concerns.
atoms: Advocacy accounts attached historical recognition material to posts about the MV Hondius cluster.
Advocacy accounts attached scientific-themed imagery to posts about the MV Hondius cluster.

sentence_level.sentence_text: In some instances, cropped graphics resembling current outbreak information cards have circulated alongside discussion of the ship cases, giving an impression of outbreak-specific authority even when the original material came from broader ivermectin promotion.
atoms: Cropped graphics resembling outbreak information cards have circulated.
These graphics gave an impression of outbreak-specific authority.
The original material for some graphics came from broader ivermectin promotion.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Editors and public health observers say that sort of packaging can shape perception quickly, especially when the underlying text is more tentative than the images imply.
atoms: Editors and public health observers say such packaging can shape perception quickly.

sentence_level.sentence_text: For now, the central medical position has not changed: supportive care is the standard response to hantavirus, and no major agency has recommended ivermectin for that use.
atoms: Supportive care is the standard response to hantavirus.
No major agency has recommended ivermectin for hantavirus.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Even so, the Hondius cluster has revived a wider argument over whether affordable repurposed medicines receive timely attention when public fear intensifies.
atoms: The Hondius cluster has revived arguments about timely attention for affordable repurposed medicines.

sentence_level.sentence_text: With online physician commentary, consumer interest and pressure on regulators all rising at the same time, ivermectin is likely to stay at the center of a heated debate over access and evidence as calls grow for faster evaluation and a clearer answer.
atoms: Online physician commentary on ivermectin is rising.
Consumer interest in ivermectin is rising.
Pressure on regulators regarding ivermectin is rising.
Calls are growing for faster evaluation of ivermectin for hantavirus.

paragraph_level: The MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak has triggered a debate on using existing drugs like ivermectin faster than developing new therapies.
Ivermectin is gaining attention as a potential hantavirus treatment due to its low cost, familiarity, and approval for parasitic infections.
U.S. and international health officials maintain that supportive care is the standard for hantavirus and have not endorsed ivermectin due to lack of clinical proof.
Some physicians argue ivermectin's anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties make it a viable candidate for hantavirus, criticizing the neglect of repurposed drugs.
Consumer demand for ivermectin is rising, with online posts advertising access outside standard prescription pathways.
Visuals and graphics linking ivermectin's medical history to the hantavirus outbreak are shaping public perception and framing the issue as a policy matter.
The central medical position remains unchanged: no major agency recommends ivermectin for hantavirus, but pressure for faster evaluation is increasing.

visual_indicators.scene_description: A close-up photo of a bronze plaque mounted on a stone wall. The plaque is a 'National Historic Chemical Landmark' for the 'Discovery of Ivermectin' by Merck & Co., Inc. It details the drug's history and William Campbell's Nobel Prize. A blue news-style banner is superimposed at the bottom.
visual_indicators.ocr_text: ACS Chemistry for Life | National Historic Chemical Landmark | Discovery of Ivermectin | Merck & Co., Inc. | Kenilworth, New Jersey | The synthesis and development of ivermectin by Merck in the 1970s and 1980s provided a breakthrough treatment against infections diseases transmitted by parasites. This discovery resulted from an international collaboration of thousands of hundreds of Merck processes to identify a promising compound. Merck scientists synthesized thousands of analogs of this lead and tested them for activity, narrowing efficacy in highly effective treatment for various parasites, disease affecting a variety of animals. Following its approval for human use in 1987, Merck established a worldwide program to donate Ivermectin as Mectizan® to treat onchocerciasis (river blindness), greatly reducing the prevalence of this debilitating disease. In 2015, Merck scientist William Campbell, shared the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for his role in developing ivermectin. | American Chemical Society | December 3, 2016 | Doctors revisit ivermectin as hantavirus questions grow
visible_overlays_annotations: Blue news banner at bottom: 'Doctors revisit ivermectin as hantavirus questions grow'

retrieved_evidence.text: U.S. health officials urged people not to use ivermectin as rumors swirl about the MV Hondius hantavirus cluster. WHO says there’s no research showing ivermectin works for hantavirus; care is supportive.

Online posts advertising access to ivermectin outside the usual prescription channels have gained visibility in recent days, which observers interpret as a sign of rising public demand. According to digital monitoring groups, various websites and social media accounts now promote mail-order options, informal teleconsultations or other pathways presented as quick routes to obtaining the drug. Public health experts say these messages often appeal to individuals seeking perceived early treatments, and note that regulators are monitoring the situation as they assess how best to respond to shifting patterns of use.

"Ivermectin might work...so I'll sell it to you."
"No prescription needed." 🤡🤦🏼‍♂️
Health: Ivermectin has anti-inflammatory, anti-viral, anti-bacterial & anti-tumor properties.

Regulatory agencies on both national and international levels are facing growing calls to accelerate the evaluation of older drugs for new uses, according to officials and policy observers. In the wake of recent outbreaks, including hantavirus clusters, advocacy groups and some medical professionals have urged authorities to study repurposed medicines such as ivermectin more quickly, while still maintaining safety safeguards. Regulators acknowledge the pressure and say they are examining options to streamline trial approvals and data review, even as they stress the need for robust methodologies and transparent decision-making.

Some physicians and a number of social media users have recently argued that ivermectin may have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that warrant closer scrutiny, according to early reports from professional forums and online discussions. These commentators suggest that the drug’s pharmacological profile could, in their view, justify further laboratory and clinical exploration in the context of certain viral illnesses. Other medical professionals adopt a more cautious tone, emphasizing that such claims should be examined through structured studies before any changes to prevailing treatment recommendations are considered.

In recent coverage of the MV Hondius hantavirus cluster, renewed attention has been drawn to ivermectin, a drug originally developed as an antiparasitic and widely recognized through institutional awards and plaques celebrating its discovery. Commentators note that this historical recognition now intersects with contemporary debates over its possible anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, as clinicians and online communities discuss whether such established, low-cost medications should be revisited as potential adjunct treatments while formal hantavirus trials and guidelines remain limited.

Health policy analysts note that ivermectin is generally described as inexpensive and widely familiar to the public, factors that contribute to its practical appeal in many countries. According to economists and clinicians, the drug’s long-standing presence in community pharmacies and its relatively low cost can make it an attractive candidate when patients or practitioners explore possible treatment options beyond newer, higher-priced medications. Observers say this perception of accessibility and affordability may help explain why the medicine features prominently in discussions about potential repurposed therapies.

According to recent reports on the MV Hondius hantavirus situation, individual physicians and medical commentators have used social platforms to argue that ivermectin’s broad anti-inflammatory and antiviral profile should not be dismissed outright. One widely shared post, presented in a formal, almost advisory tone, urged that the drug be considered for compassionate or exploratory use in severe respiratory cases linked to hantavirus, even as its efficacy remains unproven in controlled trials, illustrating how clinical opinion can influence public perception during emerging infectious disease events.

U.S. health officials, echoing guidance from global health bodies, have reiterated in recent days that supportive care remains the standard approach for managing hantavirus infections. According to statements from federal agencies, current practice focuses on careful monitoring, respiratory support where needed and timely transfer to intensive care units in severe cases. International health organizations have issued similar reminders, noting that early recognition, meticulous clinical management and adequate hospital resources are seen as crucial factors influencing patient outcomes while therapeutic research continues.

Medical commentators say there is currently no established clinical proof that ivermectin treats hantavirus infection, but describe the issue as an unresolved debate that continues to draw attention. According to infectious disease experts, available data are regarded as incomplete, and differing interpretations of preliminary findings have fueled ongoing discussion in both scientific and lay communities. Several researchers are calling for more structured trials and transparent publication of results, arguing that clearer evidence, whether confirming or refuting potential benefits, would help guide future therapeutic decisions.

In the wake of mounting concern over the MV Hondius hantavirus cluster, reports have described informal channels through which members of the public seek access to ivermectin outside standard prescription pathways. Posts advertising direct purchase or quick delivery of the drug, often framed as a precautionary measure against viral illness, have circulated as anxiety over limited treatment options grows. Health authorities and pharmacists, in recent statements, have warned that such demand can outpace evidence-based guidance, raising questions about dosing, safety, and the risks of self-medication during an evolving outbreak.

The recent cluster of hantavirus infections associated with the cruise vessel MV Hondius has been cited by commentators as a key trigger for renewed online discussion about possible treatments, according to public health observers. Social media platforms and health forums have seen a marked uptick in posts linking the incident to debates over existing medicines that could potentially be repurposed. Analysts note that many of these discussions reference the shipboard outbreak as an example of how sudden clusters can drive interest in alternative or adjunctive therapeutic options, even while formal investigations into the event are still under way.

The expedition vessel MV Hondius, operated for polar and remote destination cruising, has been cited in recent accounts as the setting for a suspected hantavirus cluster that prompted quarantine measures and heightened surveillance. Descriptions of the ship at sea, with passengers and crew following health protocols, have anchored public understanding of how infections can spread in semi-enclosed environments. This maritime context has, in turn, fuelled speculation about early treatment options such as ivermectin, as observers look to past experiences with respiratory outbreaks on ships to interpret and contextualize the current discussion.

Ivermectin, long used in human medicine for certain parasitic infections, has re-emerged in public debate in recent days amid renewed concern over viral disease treatments, according to infectious disease specialists. Health professionals note that the medicine has been part of standard therapeutic arsenals in many countries for years, particularly in the management of specific tropical and intestinal parasites. However, experts say its role outside these approved indications remains a topic of ongoing discussion in medical and regulatory circles, with further data and structured evaluations frequently being called for.

A close-up photo of a bronze plaque mounted on a stone wall. The plaque is a 'National Historic Chemical Landmark' for the 'Discovery of Ivermectin' by Merck & Co., Inc. It details the drug's history and William Campbell's Nobel Prize. A blue news-style banner is superimposed at the bottom.

ACS Chemistry for Life | National Historic Chemical Landmark | Discovery of Ivermectin | Merck & Co., Inc. | Kenilworth, New Jersey | The synthesis and development of ivermectin by Merck in the 1970s and 1980s provided a breakthrough treatment against infections diseases transmitted by parasites. This discovery resulted from an international collaboration of thousands of hundreds of Merck processes to identify a promising compound. Merck scientists synthesized thousands of analogs of this lead and tested them for activity, narrowing efficacy in highly effective treatment for various parasites, disease affecting a variety of animals. Following its approval for human use in 1987, Merck established a worldwide program to donate Ivermectin as Mectizan® to treat onchocerciasis (river blindness), greatly reducing the prevalence of this debilitating disease. In 2015, Merck scientist William Campbell, shared the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for his role in developing ivermectin. | American Chemical Society | December 3, 2016 | Doctors revisit ivermectin as hantavirus questions grow
ACS

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post accurately reports the MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak, the official stance on supportive care, the lack of endorsement for ivermectin, and the specific online advocacy and sales activities described in the evidence and images.
text_analysis.global_assessment: The text is a faithful, well-sourced summary of the current debate surrounding ivermectin and the MV Hondius outbreak, with no factual errors, distortions, or cross-modal inconsistencies.

images.scene_description: A close-up photograph of a bronze plaque titled 'National Historic Chemical Landmark: Discovery of Ivermectin' mounted on a stone wall. A digital blue news banner is overlaid at the bottom reading 'Doctors revisit ivermectin as hantavirus questions grow'.
images.ocr_text: ACS Chemistry for Life | National Historic Chemical Landmark | Discovery of Ivermectin | Merck & Co., Inc. Kenilworth, New Jersey | The synthesis and development of ivermectin by Merck in the 1970s and 1980s provided a breakthrough treatment against infections diseases transmitted by parasites. This discovery resulted from an international collaboration of thousands of hundreds of Merck processes to identify a promising compound. Merck scientists synthesized thousands of analogs of this lead and tested them for activity, narrowing efficacy in highly effective treatment for various parasites, disease affecting a variety of animals. Following its approval for human use in 1987, Merck established a worldwide program to donate Ivermectin as Mectizan® to treat onchocerciasis (river blindness), greatly reducing the prevalence of this debilitating disease. In 2015, Merck scientist William Campbell, shared the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for his role in developing ivermectin. | American Chemical Society December 3, 2016 | Doctors revisit ivermectin as hantavirus questions grow

image_analysis.global_notes: The post uses a mix of screenshots and photos. Only the first image shows clear evidence of visual editing (a news banner overlay). The other images are clean screenshots or photos.

judge.level3_rationale: The post accurately reports the MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak and the resulting debate over ivermectin, correctly reflecting official guidance and online advocacy, with only a minor visual editing element (a news banner overlay) present in one image.
judge.reasoning_trace: The text is well-aligned with evidence (SUPPORTED). The images match the text descriptions. Image 1 has a digital overlay (V2). No T1, T2, T3, V1, C1, C2, or C3 labels fired. With exactly 1 mild L2 label (V2) and no contradictions, the verdict is Mostly True.
judge.core_claim: The MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak has triggered a debate over using ivermectin, with online advocacy and sales rising despite official health agencies maintaining that supportive care is the standard and ivermectin is unproven.
key_subclaims: The MV Hondius hantavirus cluster has sparked public anxiety and debate over repurposed drugs like ivermectin.
U.S. and international health officials state supportive care is the standard for hantavirus and have not endorsed ivermectin.
Some physicians argue ivermectin's anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties make it a viable candidate for hantavirus.
Online posts are advertising access to ivermectin outside standard prescription pathways.
Visuals circulating include historical recognition material and scientific-themed imagery linked to the outbreak.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The MV Hondius hantavirus cluster has sparked public anxiety and debate over repurposed drugs like ivermectin.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms the cluster triggered renewed online discussion about possible treatments.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: U.S. and international health officials state supportive care is the standard for hantavirus and have not endorsed ivermectin.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms officials urged against use and WHO says no research shows it works; care is supportive.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Some physicians argue ivermectin's anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties make it a viable candidate for hantavirus.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms some physicians argue these properties warrant scrutiny.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Online posts are advertising access to ivermectin outside standard prescription pathways.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms posts advertising access outside usual channels have gained visibility.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Visuals circulating include historical recognition material and scientific-themed imagery linked to the outbreak.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms renewed attention drawn to ivermectin's institutional awards and plaques.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The post's core claims regarding the outbreak, official guidance, and online debate are fully supported by the evidence. The only distortion is a minor visual editing element (a news banner overlay on one image), which does not alter the factual accuracy of the text or the overall understanding of the event. This fits the 'Mostly True' criteria: core claim is true, and there is exactly one mild local issue (V2).

image_findings.explanation: The image contains a digital blue news banner overlay ('Doctors revisit ivermectin...') added to a photo of a plaque, which constitutes visual editing.
The image is a screenshot of a social media post by a physician discussing ivermectin, matching the text's description of online advocacy.
The image shows an expedition vessel at sea, consistent with the MV Hondius mentioned in the text.
The image is a screenshot of a social media post advertising ivermectin sales, matching the text's description of consumer demand.
